content.problemStatement:
Chef bought an electronic board and pen. He wants to use them to record his clients' signatures.

The board is a grid with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM) of pixels. Initially, all pixels are white. A client uses the electronic pen to sign on the board; whenever the pen touches a pixel, this pixel becomes black. Note that a signature may be non-continuous (a client may lift the pen while signing).

Chef stores a typical signature of his current client as a matrix of characters Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j​, where for each valid iii and jjj, Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j​ is either '1' (if the cell in the iii-th row and jjj-th column is black) or '0' (if this cell is white). The client just signed on the board; this signature is stored in the same form as a matrix Bi,jB_{i, j}Bi,j​. Chef wants to know how close this signature is to this client's typical signature.

Two signatures are considered the same if it is possible to choose (possibly negative) integers drdrdr and dcdcdc such that for each 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N and 1≤j≤M1 \le j \le M1≤j≤M, Ai,j=Bi+dr,j+dcA_{i, j} = B_{i + dr, j + dc}Ai,j​=Bi+dr,j+dc​. Here, if Bi+dr,j+dcB_{i + dr, j + dc}Bi+dr,j+dc​ does not correspond to a valid cell, it is considered to be '0'.

To compare the signatures, the colours of zero or more cells must be flipped in such a way that the signatures become the same (each flipped cell may be in any matrix). The error in the client's current signature is the minimum number of cells whose colours must be flipped. Find the error in the signature.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a string with length MMM describing the iii-th row of the matrix AAA.
NNN more lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains a string with length MMM describing the iii-th row of the matrix BBB.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the error in the current signature.
